ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mutations" (
  "mutation_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the mutation */,
  "gene" TEXT /* Name of the gene where the mutation occurs */,
  "mutation_type" TEXT /* Type of mutation (e.g., point mutation) */,
  "chromosome" TEXT /* Chromosome where the mutation is located */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Position of the mutation on the chromosome */,
  "sample_id" TEXT /* ID of the sample containing the mutation */,
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Cluster ID for grouping similar mutations */,
  "cellular_prevalence" REAL /* Cellular prevalence of the mutation */,
  "cellular_prevalence_std" REAL /* Standard deviation of cellular prevalence */,
  "variant_allele_frequency" REAL /* Variant allele frequency of the mutation */,
  "mutation_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the mutation */,
  "mutation_impact" TEXT /* Impact of the mutation on the gene or protein function */,
  "mutation_consequence" TEXT /* Consequence of the mutation (e.g., synonymous, missense) */,
  "disease_association" TEXT /* Association with a specific disease */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mutation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mutations_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mutations_cluster_id FOREIGN KEY ("cluster_id") REFERENCES clusters ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "sample_name" TEXT /* Name of the sample */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was collected */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the sample (e.g., patient, control) */,
  "sample_type" TEXT /* Type of sample (e.g., blood, tissue) */,
  "patient_id" TEXT /* ID of the patient from which the sample was collected */,
  "tissue_type" TEXT /* Type of tissue from which the sample was collected */,
  "sample_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the sample (e.g., good, poor) */,
  "sequencing_platform" TEXT /* Sequencing platform used to analyze the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster criteria */,
  "cluster_type" TEXT /* Type of cluster (e.g., gene-based, function-based) */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who created the cluster */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the cluster was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user account */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, administrator) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_samples" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "sample_id" TEXT /* ID of the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analyses" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis */,
  "sample_id" TEXT /* ID of the sample analyzed */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis performed */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used for the analysis */,
  "results" TEXT /* Results of the analysis */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who performed the analysis */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the analysis was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analyses_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analysis that generated the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., summary, detailed) */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Data contained in the report */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* User who generated the report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the report was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_analysis_id FOREIGN KEY ("analysis_id") REFERENCES analyses ("analysis_id")
)
column_names: Name of the gene where the mutation occurs
Chromosome where the mutation is located
Position of the mutation on the chromosome
ID of the sample containing the mutation
Cluster ID for grouping similar mutations
Variant allele frequency of the mutation
Impact of the mutation on the gene or protein function
Consequence of the mutation (e.g., synonymous, missense)
Date when the sample was collected
Source of the sample (e.g., patient, control)
ID of the patient from which the sample was collected
Type of tissue from which the sample was collected
Quality of the sample (e.g., good, poor)
Sequencing platform used to analyze the sample
Timestamp when the cluster was created
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, administrator)
Timestamp when the analysis was performed
ID of the analysis that generated the report
Timestamp when the report was generated
